data.2034: In this function, each executive device is activated and deactivated independently of the others. Such an option enables immediate verification of correct operation of particular devices: lighter, blow-in (fan), feeder, CH pump, DHW pump, embedded valves and possible additional valves. By means of blow-in power the rotational speed of the fan can be controlled.

data.2048: This function is used after fully refilling the fuel in the container, in order to refresh the amount of percentage fuel consumption to 100%.
data.2049: After pressing the Temperature Settings icon, a screen appears, enabling the user to change the settings of the requested temperatures and the hysteresis, both for the CH boiler and DHW re-boiler.
data.2050: This option is used to set the set the boilers set temperature.
data.2051: This option is used to set the hysteresis of threshold temperature of the additional CH pump. This is the difference between the threshold temperature and the deactivation temperature.
data.2052: This option is used to set the hysteresis of the set re-boiler temperature. This is the difference between the set temperature (that is, the one required on the re-boiler) and the temperature of return to operation (for example: when the set temperature has the value of 55°C and the hysteresis is 5°C. After reaching the set temperature (that is 55°C) the DHW pump is deactivated. DHW pump will turn on again when the temperature decreases to 50°C).
data.2053: After pressing the Operation Settings icon, a screen appears, enabling the user to change the settings of the boiler operation.
data.2054: This option is used to set CH and DHW pump activation temperature.
data.2055: This option is used to set the operation time of the fuel feeder. The operation time should be set depending on the kind of the fuel used.
data.2056: The pause time is used to set the fuel feeders operation pause. the pause should be adjusted to the type of fuel combusted in the boiler. Incorrect selection of the pause time may result in inadequate functioning of the boiler, i.e. the fuel cannot be completely burnt or the boiler may.
data.2057: This function controls the rotational speed of the fan. The regulation range is within 1 to 100%.
data.2058: This option is used to set the time of feeders operation and blow-in while in support mode (above the set temperature).
data.2059: This function is used to set the fuel feeding pause time when staying in the support cycle (boiler operation above the set temperature).
data.2060: In this function, the user sets the operation time and the pause time of the fan at work in the support cycle.
data.2061: This function enables programming of the weekly control for the boiler. For proper operation of this function, the current date and time must be set. In the controller, it is possible to set two types of weekly control.
data.2062: Plan of the boiler operation enables programming of the activity time of the boiler - the user may, down to 30 minutes, assign the activity periods of the boiler on selected weekdays. In the inactive periods the boiler will remain extinguished independently of the other factors (e.g.: signal from the room regulator).
data.2063: The weekly regulator function enables programming deviations of the set temperature of the boiler on particular days of the week at specific hours.
data.2064: This parameter enables clearing of all past settings of weekly control.
data.2065: This function is used for programming daily changes in the DHW set temperature. The set temperature deviations are in the range of +/-10⁰C.
data.2066: When this option is selected, the regulator switches to the house heating mode only.
data.2067: In this mode, firstly, the re-boiler pump is activated (DHW), until it reaches the DHW set temperature (valves close maximally and valve pumps are deactivated), after reaching it, the pump is deactivated and the activity of the mixing valves activates (and of the pumps - according to their settings). Valves work constantly, until the moment when the re-boiler temperature drops below the set value by the hysteresis value. Then, valve pumps are deactivated and DHW pump is activated.
data.2068: In this mode, all active pumps and valves work at the same time. Valves maintain the set temperature, and the re-boiler is heated up to the set temperature.
data.2069: In this mode the CH Valves close and shuts off the pump CO, so as not to heat the house unnecessarily, but in the case of achieving too high a temperature in the boiler (it requires activation of the return protection!), the valve will be open in emergency mode and enabled the pump CO. In this mode, the re-boiler pump and floor valves work normally according to their settings.
data.2070: The function enables turning off the floor system operation. In the case of marking this option, the controller closes all valves assigned with the floor type.
data.2071: Thermal disinfection applies to the DHW and it can be activated only in a situation, if one of the three operation modes is active: Re-Boiler Priority, Parallel pumps or Summer Mode. Thermal disinfection involves increasing the temperature to the necessary disinfection temperature (min. 65°C) throughout the entire DHW circulation.

data.2090: This parameter determines the water temperature measurement (control) frequency behind the valve to CH, or DHW installation. data.2093: It is a curve, according to which the set controller temperature is determined, on the basis of the external temperature. The more points constructing the curve, the greater its accuracy. this allows its flexible shaping.

data.2129: The GSM module is an optional device cooperating with the boiler controller, enabling remote control of the boiler operation via the use of a mobile phone. The User is notified with a text message on each alert of the boiler controller, and by sending an appropriate text message at any time, he or she receives a return message with information on the current temperature of all sensors. After entering an authentication code it is also possible to remotely change the set temperatures.
data.2130: Internet module is a device enabling remote control of the boiler over the Internet. On the home computer screen the user controls the condition of all boiler system devices and the operation of each device is presented in the animated form.   Apart from the possibility to view the temperature of every sensor, the user has the possibility of introducing changes of the set temperatures for both the pumps and the mixing valves.
data.2131: Dosage of pellets for ignition phase. Usually from 12 to 16s. It depends on burner size, chimney draft and pellet size. More pellets - longer time to create flame. However if there is not enough pellets (les than 12s), there is a risk of poor flame detection.
data.2132: This time the feeder operation during firing delay process.
data.2133: This time the feeder pause during firing delay process.
data.2134: Speed of the fan during first half of ignition phase. Exact speed depends on fan type and it is from 1 to 8%.
data.2135: Time after which shuts off the heater if it is not detected the fire on the grate.
data.2136: Adjustable time delay ignition. Its aim is to stabilize the flame around the grate.
data.2137: The power of the fan that set during the delay firing.
data.2138: The value of the fire sensor, which must be achieved in order to turn the heater off and move to delay ignition.
data.2139: Adjustable time responsible for checking whether there is fire on the grate.
data.2140: How often fan increases it's speed to clean the grate. Recommended values: 6-15min. Bigger burner / more frequent cleaning necessary.

data.2142: The parameter responsible for the upper range of the feeder work defined in seconds for 100% of the power boiler.

data.2144: The parameter responsible for the lower range of the feeder pause defined in seconds for 1% of the power boiler.

data.2146: Speed of the fan during maximum burner power.

data.2151: This parameter is used to correct operation time and pause in feeder work.

data.2153: Time after which the boiler to expire in the case even if the sensor will detect light a fire on the grate.

data.2155: The option to choose the boiler power.

data.2160: This is the temperature of the water heater priority. Operation of the fan and feeder is limited to this temperature, because it prevents overheating of the boiler.

data.2163: Maximum boiler temperature, If the temperature is higher, alarm message 'CH temperature too high' appears. When CH temperature is higher than set point, an 'Boiler overheated' alarm message appears.
data.2165: DHW set temperature, which must be reached to disinfection could run successfully.
data.2166: It is the duration of disinfection. Its progress is visible in panels screen in window responsible for the boiler temperature.

If during set time is increased DHW temperature not reached, boiler returns to normal DHW production after finishing the maximal time of disinfection. This is a safety feature to make sure, that DHW temperature doesn’t stay increased for long period of time.

data.2181: This function is used to select the type of installation: from the boiler and from the puffer.
data.2182: Applies to an installation in which the DHW tank is fed directly from the boiler.
data.2183: Applies to an installation in which the DHW tank is fed from the buffer.

data.2185: This function allows you to set the temperature set point bottom buffer.
data.2186: This parameter is used for setting the working time of the pump in the period of its activity.

data.2188: The user, thanks to this function, sets the daily cycle of activation or standstill of the pump with an accuracy of 30 minutes.
data.2189: This option informs the user about the current version of the module.
data.2190: The sensor measures the proportion of oxygen in the flue gas. On the basis of this reading and its pre-set value, the controller adjusts the operation of the flue gas fan and the fuel feeder in order to achieve the maximum efficiency of the combustion process. Lambda sensor ensures optimum efficiency and emission regardless of such variables as the quality of the fuel.
data.2191: This parameter defines the time from entering the operation mode to the first update. Lambda update is the first step to correct the oxygen amount in the flue gas.
data.2192: This parameter is used to set the frequency of Lambda sensor update. Lambda update is the first step to correct the oxygen amount in the flue gas.
data.2193: % of feeder operating time correction. This correction will aply each update time you have set in Time of regular update function.
data.2194: % of fan operating speed correction. This correction will aply each update time you have set in Time of regular update function.
data.2195: The user may decide if the blow force correction resulting from Lambda sensor measurement should be summed with the previous corrections or if it should be calculated separately.
data.2196: This parameter defines the maximum decrease in the blow force which may be introduced as a result of correction made by Lambda sensor.
data.2197: This parameter defines the maximum increase in the blow force which may be introduced as a result of correction made by Lambda sensor.
data.2198: This parameter defines the pre-set oxygen amount in the flue gas when the CH boiler operates at full power.
data.2199: This parameter defines the pre-set oxygen amount in the flue gas when the CH boiler operates at minimum power.
data.2200: This parameter defines the activation time of the additional pump which serves as the CH pump. When a selected sensor reaches this temperature, the pump is activated.

data.3330: High feeder temperature. Normal temperature should be in range from 20 to 45°C. Check following as soon as possible: - Correct burner grate position. Grate cannot be turned in opposite direction and must fit properly inside the burner head. - Clean burner grate below and above and remove all ashes. The whole burning process must be performed only on the grate. Pellets and ashes cannot touch burner head, only the grate. - Clean flue gas connection and chimney to make sure hot air is going into the chimney without any problem. Heat exchanger, chimney flue path must be clean otherwise air cannot escape out of the boiler and burner is overheated because of it. PVC hose, burner or electrical parts can be damaged by overheating, so you need to check and solve this issue immediately. - Correct fan speed during PID work. Low fan speed can influence burner temperature, fan is not cooling down the burner properly. Ask your installer for solution.
data.3331: Bad flame detection. Fotosensor detects light intensity in values lower than 200lx. It is possible that boiler stops and displayed alarm message because of that. Following situations can occur: - Failed ignition: fotosensor didn’t see the light during automatic ignition and as a result ignition was not successful. Check following to solve this issue: - Check cleanliness of fotosensors plexi-glass. If there is dust, lower light detection is possible. Remove burner pipe and clean fotosensor by hand through the hole below removed burner pipe. - Check burner connectors. Green small connector is responsible for signal from the fotosensor. Bad connection can influence fotosensor sensitivity. - Fotosensor must be fitted inside the burner body max 1cm. When fotosensor is deeper inside the burner it can lower the flame detection. - If you see above alarm message contact your installer for help.
data.3332: High chimney temperature. Temperature inside the chimney is higher than 160°C. Normal chimney temperature is below 140°C. Check following: - Flue path must be cleaned as well as heat exchange. If there is a lot of ash, hot air is escaping out of the boiler too fast. - Chimney draft. If the draft is higher than 20Pa, it creates high chimney temperature and also higher pellet consumption. Ask your installer for more information. - Ash tray position. Ash tray must be placed correctly. Ash container separates front and rear part of the boiler so hot air goes through the boiler heat exchanger corretly. If there is small distance between first smoke cooler and ash tray, air can go faster to the chimney which increases chimney temperature. Clean all ash around ashtray and try to fit the ashtray on the correct position again.
data.3333: Low chimney temperature. Chimney temperature is lower than 60°C. This creates a risk of rust inside the boiler and flue path. It is necessary to increase chimney temperature: - Open flap located on the top of turbulators inside the heat exchanger. Open cleaning doors and check flap with two bolts on it. Screw both bolts in to move flap. Chimney temperature will be increased. - If there is still low temperature during maximal power of the boiler you can increase fan speed (but too much air creates bad pellet combustion). If nothing helps, ask installer for his support. - Chimney temperature during maximal power should be over 70°C.
data.3334: Low return temperature. Return temperature is lower than 38°C for long period of time during boiler operation. Ask your installer for solution. Low return temperature can create rust inside the boiler. Low return water protection must be higher than 38°C. This is usually managed by valve which keeps the temperature above 38°C.
data.3335: Long ignition phase. Ignition takes longer than 10min. Standard ignition period is 5min. Longer ignition phase can be influenced by following: - High chimney draft. Higher chimney draft than 25Pa can lengthen ignition time. - High air flow during ignition. Installer should check fan speed during ignition inside Service menu. - High amount of pellets for ignition. Installer can reduce the number of pellets inside Service menu. - Burner grate is not cleaned properly. - Igniter life is at the end. Igniter doesn’t produce enough heat to ignite the pellets fast. - Ask your installer for solution.
data.3336: Too many ignition cycles. Our boiler is igniting too often (more than 13 times per 24hours). We recommend to ask your installer for advice. Boiler should ignite less than 10 times per one day so ignitor life is not shortened to fast. Ask your installer for solution how to decrease ignition numbers.
data.3337: Fan rotation is decreased. Fan of the burner is moving slower than it should which results in high Mosfet temperature. Check and clean fan so no ash is inside. Remove the burner out of the boiler to clean fan correctly. If this situation appears again, inform your installer and ask for solution.

data.3996: MOSFET overheating protection. Factory value is 7. MOSFET is responsible for fan speed modulation. It can be overheated when for example fan is dirty and its rotation is not as it should be. This creates higher current in the MOSFET and to protect the damage, a 'MOSFET temperature too high' message appears on the screen. Higher number means faster reaction on MOSFET overheating. 0 means this protection is deactivated. Check proper fan function when this message is shown

data.4520: When this option is selected, the regulator switches to the house heating mode only.
data.4521: In this mode, firstly, the re-boiler pump is activated (DHW), until it reaches the DHW set temperature (valves close maximally and valve pumps are deactivated), after reaching it, the pump is deactivated and the activity of the mixing valves activates (and of the pumps - according to their settings). Valves work constantly, until the moment when the re-boiler temperature drops below the set value by the hysteresis value. Then, valve pumps are deactivated and DHW pump is activated.
data.4522: In this mode, all active pumps and valves work at the same time. Valves maintain the set temperature, and the re-boiler is heated up to the set temperature.
data.4523: In this mode the CH Valves close and shuts off the pump CO, so as not to heat the house unnecessarily, but in the case of achieving too high a temperature in the boiler (it requires activation of the return protection!), the valve will be open in emergency mode and enabled the pump CO. In this mode, the re-boiler pump and floor valves work normally according to their settings.

data.4534: This parameter allows the driver to return to factory settings.

data.4536: Fire-up parameters refer to detailed settings of the fire-up process. The settings should be adjusted to the type of fuel used.
data.4537: Dosage of pellets for ignition phase. Usually from 12 to 16s. It depends on burner size, chimney draft and pellet size. More pellets - longer time to create flame. However if there is not enough pellets (les than 12s), there is a risk of poor flame detection.
data.4538: This parameter is used for setting the working time of the pump in the period of its activity.
data.4539: This parameter determines the frequency of activating the circulation pump in the periods of its activity.
data.4540: This function enables the user to adjust the blow force.
data.4541: Time after which shuts off the heater if it is not detected the fire on the grate.
data.4542: Adjustable time delay ignition. Its aim is to stabilize the flame around the grate.
data.4544: Adjustable time responsible for checking whether there is fire on the grate.
data.4545: This function is used to define how often the cleaning phase should be activated during operation.
data.4547: This parameter defines the length of the final extinguishing stage, when the fans work at full speed.
data.4548: Time after which the boiler to expire in the case even if the sensor will detect light a fire on the grate.
data.4549: The power with which the fan is working during extinguishing process.
data.4550: Parameters of this submenu enable adapting operation times of the fans and the feeder to individual conditions of the boiler.
data.4551: This function is used for controlling the fan capacity. The principle of operation of this regulation is based on moving the fan characteristics up or down.
data.4552: Feeder coefficient serves to optimize the feeder operation so that it supplies an appropriate amount of fuel. With this function the user may increase or decrease the amount of fuel fed to the CH boiler (the amount is given in percentages).
data.4553: Feeder coefficient serves to optimize the feeder operation so that it supplies an appropriate amount of fuel. With this function the user may increase or decrease the amount of fuel fed to the CH boiler (the amount is given in percentages).
data.4554: The sensor measures the proportion of oxygen in the flue gas. On the basis of this reading and its pre-set value, the controller adjusts the operation of the flue gas fan and the fuel feeder in order to achieve the maximum efficiency of the combustion process. Lambda sensor ensures optimum efficiency and emission regardless of such variables as the quality of the fuel.
data.4555: This parameter defines the time from entering the operation mode to the first update. Lambda update is the first step to correct the oxygen amount in the flue gas.
data.4556: This parameter is used to set the frequency of Lambda sensor update. Lambda update is the first step to correct the oxygen amount in the flue gas.
data.4557: % of feeder operating time correction. This correction will aply each update time you have set in Time of regular update function.
data.4558: % of fan operating speed correction. This correction will aply each update time you have set in Time of regular update function.
data.4559: The user may decide if the blow force correction resulting from Lambda sensor measurement should be summed with the previous corrections or if it should be calculated separately.
data.4560: This parameter defines the maximum decrease in the blow force which may be introduced as a result of correction made by Lambda sensor.
data.4561: This parameter defines the maximum increase in the blow force which may be introduced as a result of correction made by Lambda sensor.
data.4562: This parameter defines the pre-set oxygen amount in the flue gas when the CH boiler operates at full power.
data.4563: This parameter defines the pre-set oxygen amount in the flue gas when the CH boiler operates at minimum power.
data.4564: Subatmospheric pressure control involves the exhaust fan and it is based on the measurement read from the subatmospheric pressure sensor. This function aims at maintaining the pre-set value of the subatmospheric pressure by adjusting the exhaust fan speed.
data.4565: This function is used to define the pre-set subatmospheric pressure in the operation mode.
data.4566: This function is used to define the pre-set subatmospheric pressure in the fire-up phase.
data.4567: This function is used to define acceptable deviation of the subatmospheric pressure value.
data.4568: This parameter defines the frequency of subatmospheric pressure regulation.
data.4569: This parameter defines by how many percent the fan speed should be changed when the subatmospheric pressure is introduced.
data.4570: If the current value drops below the minimum threshold and remains on this level for the time defined as alarm delay despite introducing the subatmospheric pressure control, an emergency procedure is introduced - the subatmospheric pressure control is disabled and the display shows an appropriate message.
data.4571: If the current value exceeds the maximum threshold and remains on this level for the time defined as alarm delay despite introducing the subatmospheric pressure control, an emergency procedure is introduced - the subatmospheric pressure control is disabled and the display shows an appropriate message.
data.4572: This parameter is used to define how long the current value of the subatmospheric pressure may exceed the alarm thresholds.
data.4573: This function is used to control the pressure of water in the system.
data.4574: This parameter defines the lower threshold value of the acceptable water pressure.
data.4575: This parameter defines the upper threshold value of the acceptable water pressure.
data.4576: This parameter is used to set valve parameters.
data.4577: Using this function, the embedded valve can be calibrated at any time. During calibration, the valve is set to a safe position, that is, for the CH valve to the full open position, while for floor valve, to a closed position.
data.4578: This is a maximum single stroke (opening or closing) that the valve may make during one temperature sampling.
data.4579: The parameter determines which valve opening may be the smallest. Thanks to this parameter, the valve may be opened minimally, to maintain the smallest flow.
data.4580: Parameter specifying time needed for the servomotor of the valve to open the valve from 0% to 100% position. This time should be selected according to the owned valve servomotor.
data.4581: This parameter determines the water temperature measurement (control) frequency behind the valve to CH, or DHW installation. If the sensor indicates a change in temperature (deviation from the set value), then the electric valve will open or close by the set stroke, in order to return to the set temperature.
data.4582: By means of this setting the user selects the type of controlled valve.
data.4583: To activate the weather function, the external sensor should be placed in a non-insulated place not exposed to atmospheric impacts. After installation and connection, the sensor should have the function Weather Sensor activated in the controller menu.
data.4584: In this function, the user has the possibility of selection and configuration of operation of the room regulator.
data.4585: Mark this option when you do not want the room regulator to influence operation of the valve.

data.4587: After selecting this type of room regulator, the valve will work according to the parameters Change in the Set Valve Temperature and Difference in Room Temperatures. Activation of this room regulator enables viewing current temperature settings of the boiler, the re-boiler and the valves.
data.4588: Mark this option if the valve is to be controlled by the room two-state regulator (not equipped with RS communication).
data.4589: This function is active only in the event when either the option of TECH standard or standard valve regulator is marked. In this setting, you should set the temperature by which the valve decreases its set temperature. at the moment of achieving the set temperature at the room regulator (room heat-up).
data.4590: This setting determines a single change in the current temperature(down to 0.1°C) , at which a particular change in the set valve temperature will take place.
data.4591: This parameter allows you to change the set temperature.
data.4592: How often room thermostat checks room temperature and makes adjustment of valve operation.
data.4593: If after connection of the valve to the controller it turns out that it was to be connected the other way round, then the power supply cables do not have to be switched, but it is enough to change the opening direction in this parameter: LEFT or RIGHT.
data.4594: This function permits the setting of the boiler protection against too cool water returning from the main circulation, which could cause low-temperature corrosion in the boiler.
data.4595: The return protection operates in the way that when the temperature is too low, the valve is closed, until the short circulation of the boiler reaches the appropriate temperature.
data.4596: This option allows the user to select the working mode of a pump.
data.4597: Setting particular parameters of an additional valve is possible only after correct registration of the valve by introducing the module number.
data.4598: This option applies to the return sensor and the external sensor and enables identification of whether functioning of an additional valve is to include own sensors of the valve or the main controller sensors.
data.4599: This function is used for a complete removal of the valve from the controller memory. Valve removal is used e.g. at disassembling the valve or module replacement.
data.4601: After marking this option, the additional pump will serve as an additional CH pump. This pump will be activated if the temperature on the sensor exceeds the temperature threshold value.
data.4602: Domestic hot water pump operated by DHW temperature sensor and DHW hysteresis.
data.4603: After marking this option, an additional device will serve as the circulation pump - serving to control the pump mixing hot water between the boiler and the hot utility water receivers.
data.4604: Maintenance the controller may automatically activate cleaning and maintenance procedures.
data.4605: Periodical cleaning functions independently of the extinguishing stage and involves stopping the controller operation and enabling both fans at a pre-defined speed.
data.4606: When this function is selected, an additional menu with specific ash removal parameters appears in the main menu.

data.4609: The burner fan increases its speed during grid cleaning periods. This cleaning is semi-automatic. Remember that manual burner cleaning is also always required.
data.4610: In this function, the user has the possibility of selection and configuration of operation of the room regulator 2.
data.4611: This option should be selected if the room regulator connected to the controller uses RS communication. Such a regulator enables the user to view current CH boiler parameters and change some of the settings e.g. the pre-set temperature of the CH boiler or the pre-set DHW temperature.
data.4612: If the controller has been connected to a two-state regulator, the standard regulator option should be marked. Such a type of room regulator enables the sending of information to the main controller about additional heating or under heating of the room.
data.4613: If you mark this option, the signal from the room regulator about additional heating of the room will result in turning off the CH pump.
data.4614: After marking this option signal from the room regulator about additional heating of the room will result in activation of the firing upprocess in the boiler.
data.4615: This function is used for setting the current time. Setting this function is necessary for correct operation of the weekly control.
data.4616: The correct conduct of the fuel calibration process will enable current view of the current fuel level on the controller display.
data.4617: The GSM module is an optional device cooperating with the boiler controller, enabling remote control of the boiler operation via the use of a mobile phone. The User is notified with a text message on each alert of the boiler controller, and by sending an appropriate text message at any time, he or she receives a return message with information on the current temperature of all sensors. After entering an authentication code it is also possible to remotely change the set temperatures.
data.4618: Internet module is a device enabling remote control of the boiler over the Internet. On the home computer screen the user controls the condition of all boiler system devices and the operation of each device is presented in the animated form.   Apart from the possibility to view the temperature of every sensor, the user has the possibility of introducing changes of the set temperatures for both the pumps and the mixing valves.

data.10448: Scenes do not work automatically - they have to be initiated by the user. However, you can initiate a given Scene at a specific time by adding it to the Automation tasks which you will configure in the next step.
data.10449: Each Scene may comprise many actions. An action may involve a change in the device status (e.g. switching a relay) or a temperature change in a thermostat.
data.10450: Scenes enable the user to change the status of many devices with just one click. For example, you can create a Scene called "Morning", which will open all the roller shutters in your house with one click.
data.10454: How are Scenes different from Automations?

data.10458: Having administrator rights is necessary for adding more Automations as well as editing or removing them (your account has administrator privileges). In order to do it, go to the tab
data.10460: in the field of a given Automation  or “Add a Automation” in the bottom right-hand corner.
data.10461: To add another Automation, click the "Add a Automation" or "Next step" button to proceed.
data.10462: A Scenes is only a group change of status, which requires user intervention in the application (the Play button). In order for Scenes to run automatically, you must create an Automation with a given Scene.
data.10463: Each Automation has a section called "If", which defines the conditions for its activation (e.g. every day at 6:00 p.m. or when the status of the device changes), and "Then", in which we define how a given device should react when these conditions are met.
data.10464: Automations, in other words, are the moments when Scenes are activated on a regular basis or the status of a device changes. If you want a given Scene to be activated every day at 8:00 a.m., in addition to creating the Scene itself, you need to create an Automation which will run this Scene at the specified time.

data.16239: Set the CH hysteresis. CH hysteresis is the difference between the temperature at which the boiler enters minimum power and the temperature at which it returns to maximum power. For example, if the set temperature is 70°C and the CH hysteresis is 15°C, then the boiler power will be reduced after reaching the temperature of 70°C, while the return to the maximum power will take place after the temperature drops to 55°C). If the "Firing up function" is active, the boiler will be turned off after reaching the set temperature and will be fired up after it drops by the hysteresis value.

data.16637: Setting weekly schedules for the boiler and hot water heating. The boiler then switches on the heating at precisely defined time periods.
data.16638: The boiler"s operating schedule means that you can activate the heating of your house at a precisely set time. This is especially suitable in situations where you have a precise regime that is regular and you know when you are in the house and when you are not. This way, the boiler does not have to heat the pellets unnecessarily when you are not present in the building.

You can change parameters of Disinfection in Service menu, Disinfection.

data.16769: Activation of electromagnetic valve 1 for burner cleaning.
data.16770: Total time of one cleaning period. Usually from 1 to 3 min, higher burner power longer Cleaning period must be. During this period solenoid valve is opening and closing by set values in "Opening time" and "Cycle time" submenus. If Compressor is set to ON, so it cleans also during PID work, we recommend to select this value to 1min max. If you have longer compressor cleaning than that, after 30s boiler goes to automatic ignition, because there is no flame inside the boiler, but there is still 30s left for compressor cleaning - so there is a risk that compressor will blow out all supplied pellets for ignition.
data.16771: "Opening time" of solenoid valve. Valve is opened periodically during "Cleaning period". Usually 1-2s.
data.16772: Waiting time for next "Opening time" of solenoid valve. Each cleaning period the solenoid valve is opened (Opening time) periodically and "Pause time" is the waiting time for next opening (to pressurize the compressor again).
data.16773: Waiting time for another "Cleaning period". From 10 to 20 hours. This function works only during PID work, so only if compressor is On.
data.16774: Compressor cleaning 2 refers to the boiler exchanger cleaning solenoid valve.
data.16775: When activated, compressor is cleaning after extinction and during operation according the time in "Pause time" submenu. When you clean during operation, burner loses its flame after compressor cleaning, so after 30s with no flame boiler goes to automatic ignition. For this reason we recommend to clean during operation only with big powers (80kW or higher) or if boiler is running without reaching set CH temperature, so it never stops.
data.16776: Total time of one cleaning period. Usually from 1 to 3 min, higher burner power longer Cleaning period must be. During this period solenoid valve is opening and closing by set values in "Opening time" and "Cycle time" submenus. If Compressor is set to ON, so it cleans also during PID work, we recommend to select this value to 1min max. If you have longer compressor cleaning than that, after 30s boiler goes to automatic ignition, because there is no flame inside the boiler, but there is still 30s left for compressor cleaning - so there is a risk that compressor will blow out all supplied pellets for ignition.
data.16777: "Opening time" of solenoid valve. Valve is opened periodically during "Cleaning period". Usually 1-2s.
data.16778: Waiting time for next "Opening time" of solenoid valve. Each cleaning period the solenoid valve is opened (Opening time) periodically and "Pause time" is the waiting time for next opening (to pressurize the compressor again).
data.16779: Waiting time for another "Cleaning period". From 10 to 20 hours. This function works only during PID work, so only if compressor is On.

data.16782: Total time of one cleaning period. Usually from 1 to 3 min, higher burner power longer Cleaning period must be. During this period solenoid valve is opening and closing by set values in "Opening time" and "Cycle time" submenus. If Compressor is set to ON, so it cleans also during PID work, we recommend to select this value to 1min max. If you have longer compressor cleaning than that, after 30s boiler goes to automatic ignition, because there is no flame inside the boiler, but there is still 30s left for compressor cleaning - so there is a risk that compressor will blow out all supplied pellets for ignition.
data.16783: "Opening time" of solenoid valve. Valve is opened periodically during "Cleaning period". Usually 1-2s.
data.16784: Waiting time for next "Opening time" of solenoid valve. Each cleaning period the solenoid valve is opened (Opening time) periodically and "Pause time" is the waiting time for next opening (to pressurize the compressor again).
data.16785: Waiting time for another "Cleaning period". From 10 to 20 hours. This function works only during PID work, so only if compressor is On.
data.16786: Compressor cleaning 4 refers to the boiler exchanger cleaning solenoid valve.
data.16787: Activation and deactivation of compressor cleaning of the exchanger.
data.16788: Total time of one cleaning period. Usually from 1 to 3 min, higher burner power longer Cleaning period must be. During this period solenoid valve is opening and closing by set values in "Opening time" and "Cycle time" submenus. If Compressor is set to ON, so it cleans also during PID work, we recommend to select this value to 1min max. data.16789: "Opening time" of solenoid valve. Valve is opened periodically during "Cleaning period". Usually 1-2s.
data.16790: Waiting time for next "Opening time" of solenoid valve. Each cleaning period the solenoid valve is opened (Opening time) periodically and "Pause time" is the waiting time for next opening (to pressurize the compressor again).
data.16791: Waiting time for another "Cleaning period". From 10 to 20 hours. This function works only during PID work, so only if compressor is On.
data.16792: Compressor cleaning 5 refers to the solenoid valve for cleaning the boiler exchanger.
data.16793: Activation and deactivation of compressor cleaning of the exchanger.
data.16794: Total time of one cleaning period. Usually from 1 to 3 min, higher burner power longer Cleaning period must be. If Compressor is set to ON, so it cleans also during PID work, we recommend to select this value to 1min max. If you have longer compressor cleaning than that, after 30s boiler goes to automatic ignition, because there is no flame inside the boiler, but there is still 30s left for compressor cleaning - so there is a risk that compressor will blow out all supplied pellets for ignition.
data.16795: "Opening time" of solenoid valve. Valve is opened periodically during "Cleaning period". Usually 1-2s.
data.16796: Waiting time for next "Opening time" of solenoid valve. Each cleaning period the solenoid valve is opened (Opening time) periodically and "Pause time" is the waiting time for next opening (to pressurize the compressor again).
data.16797: Waiting time for another "Cleaning period". From 10 to 20 hours. This function works only during PID work, so only if compressor is On.
data.16798: Removes ash from the boiler to the ash container. Extends time for manual cleaning. Indicated by icon on the main screen.
data.16799: Activation or deactivation of automatic ash removal if installed in the boiler.
data.16800: How long is the ash removal motor active. Works periodically. Number should be set from 5 to 20 min accordingly to the burner size and number of ashes created. Bigger burner you have the longer operation time you need to clean all ashes.
data.16801: How long it waits without operating. It depends on pellet quality and burner size. Number could be anything from 2 to 20 hours.
data.16802: Use vacuum transport to transfer pellets from silo to the boiler room.
data.16803: Set the time when vacuum transport is activated during the day.
data.16804: Set how long vacuum transport works each day. Pellet hopper should be filled during this time, which usually takes 30min to 1h. We recommend to measure how long it takes to fill up the hopper in your exact situation and select the value according to that.
data.16805: Set the time when vacuum transport is activated during the day. Vacuum transport will then work for period of time set in "Operation time" submenu.
data.16806: Set the second time when vacuum transport is activated during the day. Use it if you need to fill up the pellet hopper more than once in a day.
data.16807: Set how long it takes to fill up the vacuum container. It is one cycle of vacuum suction. It mainly depends on the PVC tube installation so check by yourself how long it takes to fill it completely. It takes approximately 80 to 200s.
data.16808: Time when vacuum container flap is opened to release all pellets from vacuum container. We recommend to set 20s. Vacuum transport is then activated again and works for period of time set in "Opening time" submenu. Electric switch on the vacuum container must be switched on by flap to make this system work again. If flap stays opened by the level of pellets inside the hopper, vacuum transport is deactivated and waits until pellet level drops down so the flap can close again and electric contact is switched on again.
data.16809: Select exhaust fan operation speed for min and max burner power. Exhaust fan modulates its speed according the burner power and helps to boost up chimney draft. Fan speed modulation is automatic, inside the range you select.
data.16810: Activation or deactivation of the exhaust fan, if installed in the boiler.
data.16811: Min speed of exhaust fan during minimal burner power.
data.16812: Max speed of exhaust fan during maximal burner power.
data.16813: Cascade controller is additional device which allows you to operate up to 4 boilers as it would be only one big boiler and use power of each boiler only when needed.
data.16814: Enable or disable the cascade controller.
data.16815: Select type of operation mode. You can operate boilers by boiler temperature or by external temperature sensor.
data.16816: Cascade operation is based on outside weather sensor. You can select from which outside temperature level your boilers starts to operate.
data.16817: Cascade controller operates the boiler to achieve selected boiler temperature.
data.16818: Select number of boilers connected into the Cascade controller.
data.16823: Hysteresis is the boiler temperature drop when boiler should be started again. If boiler achieves its maximal CH temperature, it waits set temperature drop before it starts to operate again.
data.16824: Minimal boiler CH temperature. If temperature is not reached during the time set in "Waiting time" submenu, next boiler is activated.
data.16825: Works with Modulation mode. CH temperature when selected boiler should be started.
data.16826: Works with Modulation mode. CH temperature when selected boiler should be started.
data.16827: Works with Modulation mode. CH temperature when selected boiler should be started.
data.16828: Select time how long it takes to start second/third/ forth boiler if CH temperature is not reached during selected period of time.
data.16830: Actual firmware version of cascade controller.
data.16831: To regulate combustion by measuring remaining oxygen inside the boiler.
data.16832: Activation or deactivation of the lambda probe.
data.16833: Lambda sensor is able to measure remaining (not burned) oxygen inside the boiler in range of +-1%. Short period peaks in oxygen measurement are normal, because of dosage of new pellets, chimney draft or because of burner cleaning by fan.
data.16834: How often lambda corrects the flame. This value should be around 5min. Bigger the burner size is the bigger the update time must be.
data.16835: % of feeder operating time correction. This correction will apply each update time you have set in Time of regular update function.
data.16836: % of fan operating speed correction. This correction will apply each update time you have set in Time of regular update function.
data.16837: When deactivated - Feeder and Fan corrections will be still the same (no increase or decrease after time). When activated - Feeder and Fan corrections will be +100% each "Time of regular update" until it reaches Min and Max correction which is the limit.
data.16838: This is the minimal limit when Feeder and Fan corrections can go if "Sum" function is active.There is a risk, that lambda sensor will decrease boiler power if this value is too big. If this is the case and corrected flame by lambda sensor is small, you need to change this number closer to 0%.
data.16839: This is the maximum limit when Feeder and Fan corrections can go if "Sum" function is active.If flame is too smoky, black smoke coming out of the chimney, the % f correction is too big and lambda is forcing to supply too many pellets inside the burner. If this is the case, change this number closer to 0%. You have to check the lambda oxygen detection. If there is high level of oxygen inside the boiler during long period of time, there is some leakage on the boiler body where secondary air goes inside and increases oxygen level inside the boiler. Check tightness of all doors and connections on the boiler.
data.16840: Excess oxygen measured by lambda sensor during maximal burner power.
data.16841: Excess oxygen measured by lambda sensor during minimal burner power. Lambda sensor is able to measure remaining (not burned) oxygen inside the boiler in range of +-1%. Short period peaks in oxygen measurement are normal, because of dosage of new pellets, chimney draft or because of burner cleaning by fan.
data.16842: Shows a record of temperatures over time.
data.16843: This function blocks the boiler start (firing up) if the outside temperature measured by the outside sensor is higher than the one set in the Set temperature function. Note: the outside sensor must be connected, otherwise an error message will be displayed.
data.16844: Activation and deactivation of the heating block based on the specified temperature.
data.16845: If there is lower outside temperature than set point, boiler will not operate until the outside temperature increase above set point.
data.16846: Setting the time and date for the functions of the control unit and the online system. Important for recording alarms at the exact time.
data.16849: Do you want to reset all parameters form Fitters menu back to the factory values?
data.16850: Auger anf fan operation parameters, safety values. Only installer can access this menu.
data.16851: Change feeder and fan parameters of min and max burner power for all 3 main operation phases.
data.16853: Cleaning interval before ignition. From 30s to 1 min. This feature is connected with "Blow by gear" feature.
data.16854: Speed of the cleaning before ignition. This feature is connected with "Blow out time" feature.
data.16855: Dosage of pellets for ignition phase. Usually from 12 to 16s. It depends on burner size, chimney draft and pellet size. More pellets - longer time to create flame. However if there is not enough pellets (les than 12s), there is a risk of poor flame detection.
data.16856: Delay of feeder operation for ignition. It should be the same as "Blow out time" function. When fan stops cleaning feeder supplies pellets on the grate.
data.16857: Internal feeder operation during ignition to supply remaining pellets on the grate during ignition. Usually 3min.
data.16858: Waiting time of internal feeder during ignition phase.
data.16859: Speed of the fan during first half of ignition phase. Exact speed depends on fan type and it is from 1 to 8%.
data.16860: Speed 2 of the fan during second half of ignition phase. Exact speed depends on fan type and it is from 3 to 16%.
data.16861: Maximum ignition time. 12min. Burner tries to ignite twice. If the ignition process is not successful (no flame) for the second time it shows alarm message "Heater error".
data.16862: Sensitivity of fotosensor. Lower number means higher sensitivity. If photo sensor sees the light even if there is no flame inside the boiler - increase the number to decrease the sensitivity. If photo sensor doesn"t see the light even if there is flame in the boiler - decrease the number to increase sensitivity. You can see real time light measurement on the left bottom corner of the screen. This number should be from 1000 to 5000 during PID work.
data.16863: Fan operation delay to heat up the igniter. There is 30s waiting time from the ignition start to heat up igniter. After 30s fan is started.
data.16864: Change parameters of feeder and fan for PID work, at minimal and maximal power. Inside this range (min to max power) is fan and feeder operation changed automatically by PID work.
data.16865: Change fan and external feeder operation during minimum burner power. Keep in mind that flame must be stable during this stage, so there is no risk of extinction or poor flame detection. If you make min. power flame too small, Burner cleaning can cause extinction and forced ignition because of that!
data.16866: Minimum operating time for the external feeder during minimum power of the boiler.
data.16867: Maximum feeding pause during minimum burner power. Keep in mind that flame must be big enough so photo sensor can see the light. If the number is too big (long pause between dosages of pellets at minimal power), there is a risk of poor light detection.
data.16868: Speed of the fan during minimum burner power. If the speed of the fan is too fast at minimal power, there is a risk that flame will be destroyed by to fast fan speed. The number is usually close to 20% of fan speed.
data.16870: Maximum operating time for the external feeder during maximum power of the boiler.
data.16871: Minimum feeding pause for external feeder during maximum burner power.
data.16872: Speed of the fan during minimum burner power. If the speed of the fan is too fast at minimal power, there is a risk that flame will be destroyed by to fast fan speed. The number is usually close to 20% of fan speed.
data.16873: Setting parameters for minimum boiler output.
data.16874: Minimum operating time for the external feeder during minimum power of the boiler.
data.16875: Maximum feeding pause during minimum burner power. Keep in mind that flame must be big enough so photo sensor can see the light. If the number is too big (long pause between dosages of pellets at minimal power), there is a risk of poor light detection.
data.16876: Speed of the fan during minimum burner power. If the speed of the fan is too fast at minimal power, there is a risk that flame will be destroyed by to fast fan speed. The number is usually close to 20% of fan speed.
data.16877: Setting parameters for maximum boiler output.
data.16878: Maximum operating time for the external feeder during maximum power of the boiler.
data.16879: Minimum feeding pause for external feeder during maximum burner power.
data.16880: Speed of the fan during minimum burner power. If the speed of the fan is too fast at minimal power, there is a risk that flame will be destroyed by to fast fan speed. The number is usually close to 20% of fan speed.
data.16881: Setting the boiler modulation mode. That is, how the boiler should regulate its output during operation.
data.16882: The speed of boiler output modulation. If the boiler adjusts its output, this function can influence how strongly it can intervene in the output at a given time. If there is a higher risk of overheating, for example when the volume of water in the system is low, we can determine a higher output jump so that we can reduce the output quickly and prevent the boiler from overheating or its constant switching off and on again.
data.16883: The limit at which the boiler should start modulating its output so as not to exceed the desired boiler temperature. For example, if the CH temperature is set to 75°C, then the distance from the set temperature can be, for example, 10°C. The boiler will start reducing its output when it reaches 75 - 10 = 65°C.
data.16884: Forced cleaning of the burner. When time set, boiler will stop operation and force Extinction phase. When extinction is finished, burner ignites pellets automatically again. It is good to activate this feature for bigger burners, such as 100-200kW. Recommended time is 20h.
data.16885: How often is the flame detection checked. If there is no light detected by photo sensor during certain period of time, boiler goes from operation to Ignition automatically. Standard number is between 20 to 50s.
data.16886: Extinguishing phase and operation of feeders and fans in this operating state. Varies according to burner size.
data.16887: Speed of the fan during extinction, to clean and cool down the grate.
data.16888: Operation time of internal feeder during extinction to supply all remaining pellets on the grate.
data.16889: Pause in the operation of the internal torch feeder. Linked to the Feed time function.
data.16890: Maximum extinction time when there is flame detected by photosensor. If there is no flame control unit jumps to "Delay time" function to clean the grate.
data.16891: This is cleaning and cooling of the grate after flame is gone. This time depends on burner size. Bigger burner you have longer the time must be. From 5 to 15min.
data.16892: Internal feeder works longer time than external feeder. The time extension is set in this function. Usually 100% longer operating time.
data.16893: In this item you will find settings for all the main temperatures with which the boiler operates. Both operating and safety temperatures, for example against boiler overheating.
data.16894: Maximum temperature in the chimney flue path. If the temperature is too high, boiler reacts by decreasing the burner power. Actual chimney temperature is indicated by icon on the main screen. Check recommended maximal chimney temperature of your boiler in chapter Main parameters.
data.16895: Maximum temperature in the chimney flue path. If the temperature is too high, boiler reacts by decreasing the burner power. Actual chimney temperature is indicated by icon on the main screen. Check recommended maximal chimney temperature of your boiler in chapter Main parameters.
data.16896: CH pump is activated during alarm status until CH boiler temperature doesn"t drop below set point.
data.16897: This is maximal CH temperature when DHW production has been activated in Main settings, Working modes. To be sure, that hot CH temperature doesn’t flow inside HW tank it is set, that CH temperature during DHW production is max 75°C. When "Priority temperature" (CH temp.) during DHW production is reached, boiler is turned off.
data.16898: Maximum boiler temperature, If the temperature is higher, alarm message "CH temperature too high" appears. When CH temperature is higher than set point, an "Boiler overheated" alarm message appears.
data.16899: CH boiler temperature when pumps are activated. When CH temperature is lower than set point, pumps are deactivated to protect boiler against low temperature corrosion and to save some energy.
data.16900: Select the minimum CH temperature which is possible to set in Main settings, "CH temperature" function. To protect the boiler against cold water which can create air condensation inside the boiler
data.16901: By how much can be the desired CH temperature overreached before burner goes to Extinction phase and is stopped afterwards. This feature is connected with "CH temperature" function in Main settings. Boiler is trying to keep CH temperature in range from desired CH temperature to +5°C without turning off. When set CH temperature +5°C is reached, boiler goes to Extinction.
data.16902: CH temperature minus "Boiler hysteresis" means level of temperature when boiler is activated again. CH temperature = 75°C, Boiler hysteresis = 15°C. Boiler will be activated automatically again when CH temp. drops below 60°C.
data.16903: Select minimal CH temperature which will be possible to select in Main settings, "CH temperature.
data.16904: During this time the minimal CH temperature set in "Boiler alarm min temperature" function must be reached. If the CH temperature doesn’t reach selected value within specified time, you get alarm message "Temperature rise failed". This is a boiler protection when for example there are no pellets inside the hopper, or other possible situations to double secure proper boiler function.
data.16905: Factory setup of the fan. No need to change this value. For producer only.
data.16906: Speed of the fan during minimum burner power. If the speed of the fan is too fast at minimal power, there is a risk that flame will be destroyed by to fast fan speed. The number is usually close to 20% of fan speed.
data.16907: Speed of the fan during maximum burner power.
data.16908: Change how big is the modulation change diring PID work phase. Bigger number - higher is one modulation change. Lowe number - smaller is one modulation change
data.16909: Forced increase of DHW temperature to disinfect bacteria inside DHW circuit.
data.16910: Select temperature to which boiler increases its level to disinfect DHW heating circuit. Connected with "Disinfection time" function.
data.16911: How long the DHW temperature stays increased. Connected with "Disinfection temperature" function.
data.16912: Maximal safety time of how long can be DHW temperature increased to selected value in "Disinfection temperature" function. If during set time is increased DHW temperature not reached, boiler returns to normal DHW production after finishing the maximal time of disinfection. This is a safety feature to make sure, that DHW temperature doesn’t stay increased for long period of time.

data.16928: Delay of the sensor response to detecting a change in the pellet level. data.17300: Explore personalized dashboards that clearly present key data and help you quickly understand and respond to changes in the system.
data.17301: Manage the building structure, floors, and rooms. Organize devices into logical groups, navigate the building easily, and control all devices from one place.
data.17302: Run scenes to change the state of multiple devices at once. Instantly adjust lighting, temperature, and other settings with a single action.
data.17303: Build powerful automations that adjust settings based on time schedules and the states of other devices. This is the heart of the system that keeps everything working automatically.
data.17304: Create schedules for temperature, heating curves, and relays. Automatically adjust system behavior based on time and environmental conditions for full control and comfort.
data.17305: Browse statistics and reports related to devices. Analyze temperature and other system metrics.
data.17306: System notification center. Review alerts, warnings, and information about system events such as device state changes, errors, or important messages.
data.17307: Explore all articles and user manuals to learn everything about the Sinum system.
data.17308: Configure the system and user accounts. Manage application settings, user preferences, permissions, integrations, and all key configuration options.
data.17309: Browse system and device logs. You can filter logs by type, date, device, and other parameters.
data.17311: Search for devices, scenes, automations, schedules, and other system elements using the built-in search. Quickly find what you need without navigating through multiple pages.
data.17312: View alerts, warnings, and system event information such as device state changes, errors, and important messages.
data.17313: Manage your account and personal settings. You can also log out from here.
data.17314: Open the application help page. It contains articles and guides covering every section of the application.

data.17319: Congratulations! You have completed the walkthrough.
data.17320: That's it! Finish this tutorial and go to settings. Remember, you can reset any completed tutorials in your Profile settings under the "Settings" tab.
data.17322: After navigating to the selected sections, a submenu is displayed. In the Settings section, you can fully configure the system, as well as add and delete resources such as rooms, floors, automations, scenes, schedules, and many others.
data.17323: You can always go up one level by clicking the main section icon or selecting the main menu that leads to the dashboard.
data.17324: You can perform backups of your central device. Remember to do this especially before making major changes. This way, in case of problems, you will be able to easily return to the last working settings.
data.17325: In update center you can update different elements of the system - both individual devices and the entire Sinum central. Always return here when you receive information about a new update available.
data.17327: System tab contains advanced settings for the central device. Here you can change the time, date, and location of the device, as well as configure network settings. Timer management, environmental variables, and adding integration clients (HTTP, Modbus, MQTT) are also available. You can generate tokens for integration with external systems, format the memory card, and enter or change the network settings.
data.17328: The tab with the list of your devices is divided by connection type, which significantly simplifies managing multiple devices. From this tab, you can also register new devices, however, the recommended approach is to register them from the Rooms tab.
data.17329: In the dashboard settings section, you can create and configure dashboards for yourself as well as for other system users.
data.17330: In this section, you can create floors and rooms, as well as add devices from the perspective of individual rooms. This is the recommended way to add devices to the Sinum system. You will be taken to this section after completing this tutorial.
data.17331: In this section, you can create scenes and automations. Two types of editors are available: a standard builder (intended for less advanced users) and a Lua editor (for developers). While creating scenes and automations, you have access to the full API documentation in PDF format, giving you complete control over every element of the system. Additionally, scenes and automations can be organized by grouping them into folders.
data.17332: Automations, just like scenes, can be created in two ways. The key difference is that automations include an additional trigger condition - such as a specific time or a change in the state of a device. Scenes, on the other hand, are triggered directly by the user or by the system, for example as part of another automation.
data.17333: In the Schedules section, you can easily manage the operation of thermostats and relays from a weekly perspective. In the same section, you can also create a heating curve that defines how the heating system operates.
data.17334: Statistics allow you to view historical data from all devices using customizable queries. Each query can combine multiple data sources, making it easier to analyze relationships within the system.
data.17335: In the Notifications tab, you can choose which types of events are reported to the notification system. These settings can be customized individually for each user.
data.17336: In the Profile tab, you can manage your account and personal settings. This section allows you to change your password and login, as well as select the application language and customize the interface appearance.
data.17337: In the Users tab, you can manage user accounts and permissions. You can also create new users and assign them roles. You can also view the login history of users.
data.17338: That"s all! You have completed the walkthrough of the Settings section. Now click "Rooms" to add your first room.
